defs: On the way; agoing; as, to be agate; to set the bells agate.

records.ety: [F. agate, It. agata, L. achates, fr. Gr. <?/.]

defs: A semipellucid, uncrystallized variety of quartz, presenting various tints in the same specimen. Its colors are delicately arranged in stripes or bands, or blended in clouds.
notes: ☞ The fortification agate, or Scotch pebble, the moss agate, the clouded agate, etc., are familiar varieties.

defs: A kind of type, larger than pearl and smaller than nonpareil; in England called ruby.
notes: ☞ This line is printed in the type called agate.

defs: A diminutive person; so called in allusion to the small figures cut in agate for rings and seals.
A tool used by gold-wire drawers, bookbinders, etc.; -- so called from the agate fixed in it for burnishing.
